rules: Provides Colorless Colorless energy. Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.
Switch 1 of your Benched Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Remove up to 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for any Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.
Choose 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and discard it.
Draw 2 cards.
Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your own Pokémon in order to remove up to 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.
Discard your hand, then draw 7 cards.
Look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.
Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.

Attach PlusPower to your Active Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard PlusPower. If this Pokémon's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), the attack does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.
Shuffle 2 of the other cards from your hand into your deck in order to draw a card.
Your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.
Trade 1 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile.
Attach Defender to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard Defender. Damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard 1 Energy card attached to 1 of your own Pokémon in order to choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and up to 2 Energy cards attached to it. Discard those Energy cards.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)
Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.
You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.
Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.
Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.
Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to search your deck for any card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.
Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.effect: Remove a number of damage counters from Venonat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If Venonat has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to seaweed. These vines shake as it walks.
An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Water Energy card attached to Starmie in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Starmie.

attacks.name: Star Freeze
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value this core as a gem.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of opponent's attacks still happen.)

flavorText: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.effect: If Spearow was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Spearow to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speeds to stay airborne.

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Rhyhorn during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.
Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwag but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Nidoran ♂ or Nidoran ♀ and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.
Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.
Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname "Water Queen."

attacks.name: Sleeping Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Gastly in order to use this attack. If a Pokémon Knocks Out Gastly during your opponent's next turn, Knock Out that Pokémon.

flavorText: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.

flavorText: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Puts enemies to sleep, then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.
Lives about three feet underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.

attacks.name: Snivel
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Cubone during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.

flavorText: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmander in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Obviously prefers hot places. If it gets caught in the rain, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.effect: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Bellsprout and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: A carnivorous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spits out poisonpowder to immobilize the enemy, and then finishes the enemy with a spray of acid.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Wartortle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Often hides in water to stalk unweary prey. When swimming quickly, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.
The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.
In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.

attacks.name: Ram
attacks.effect: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.effect: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavorText: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

attacks.name: Pounce
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Persian during your next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

attacks.name: Bonemerang
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Call for Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fighting Basic Pokémon card and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to K.O. targets.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Magmar in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.effect: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.effect: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

attacks.name: Tongue Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a stinging sensation when it licks enemies.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Kadabra in order to use this attack. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.

flavorText: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Merely by meditating, the Pokémon launches a powerful psychic energy attack.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: When the bulb on its back grows large, the Pokémon seems to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.effect: You can't use this attack unless the Defending Pokémon is Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.
Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Fearow.

flavorText: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

attacks.name: Leek Slap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. Either way, you can't use this attack again as long as Farfetch'd stays in play (even putting Farfetch'd on the Bench won't let you use it again).

flavorText: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. This sprig is used much like a metal sword.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch Exeggutor with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Big Eggsplosion
attacks.effect: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy attached to Exeggutor. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.
Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently, when a small colony was found living underwater.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dodrio.

flavorText: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon in order to use this attack.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Butterfree has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Arcanine in order to use this attack.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Acid
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your next turn.

flavorText: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

attacks.name: Venom Powder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

attacks.name: Irongrip
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing its victim around and toss it hard.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mirror Move
attacks.effect: If Pidgeotto was attacked last turn, do the final result of that attack on Pidgeotto to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Very protective of its sprawling territory, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

attacks.name: Meditate
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If interrupted while miming, it will slap around the enemy with its broad hands.

attacks.name: Fetch
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Comet Punch
attacks.effect: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is three years old.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrode does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stores electrical energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunderpunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A team of Diglett triplets. It triggers huge earthquakes by burrowing 60 miles underground.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Flies at high speed and attacks using the large, venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy cards attached to Zapdos in order to use this attack.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon said to appear from clouds while wielding enormous lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.
This plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Scyther's Slash attack's base damage is 60 instead of 30.

flavorText: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one of it.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Raichu.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, Raichu does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high-voltage power.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwrath but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

flavorText: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breaststroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.effect: Unless this attack Knocks Out the Defending Pokémon, return the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your opponent's hand.

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Ninetales in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

attacks.name: Boyfriends
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Nidoking you have in play.

flavorText: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 30 damage and Nidoking does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavorText: Uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break its prey's bones.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.effect: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Mewtwo in order to use this attack. During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mewtwo.

flavorText: A scientist created this Pokémon after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA engineering experiments.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage to each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: Formed by several Magnemites linked together. It frequently appears when sunspots flare up.
While seeming to do nothing, it fires punches in lightning-fast volleys that are impossible to see.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefairy's type is still Colorless.)

flavorText: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

attacks.name: Metronome
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Clefable's type is still Colorless.)

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.effect: All damage done by attacks to Clefable during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A timid Fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard in order to use this attack.

flavorText: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to unintentionally cause forest fires.

attacks.name: Scrunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Chansey during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.effect: Chansey does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to catch it.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Blastoise but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high-speed tackles.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its brain can outperform a supercomputer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5000.
